title: Presentations & Public Speaking
topics: presentation structure · signposting · visual aids · Q&A
description: Jak strukturovat a přednést prezentaci v angličtině — jazyk, signposting a Q&A.
notes: ✓ Opening: 'Good morning. Today I'm going to talk about...'
✓ Overview: 'I've divided my presentation into three parts.'
✓ Moving on: 'Let's move on to...' / 'Turning now to...'
✓ Emphasising: 'What I'd like to highlight here is...'
✓ Graph language: 'As you can see from this slide...'
✓ Closing: 'To sum up...' / 'I'd like to conclude by saying...'
✓ Q&A: 'That's a good question. Could you clarify what you mean by...?'

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.text: Good morning, everyone. Today I'd like to talk about remote working trends.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.text: I've divided my talk into three parts: the current situation, key benefits, and challenges.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.text: As you can see from this slide, 58% of workers now prefer a hybrid model.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.text: To sum up: remote work is here to stay, but the office still has a role.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.text: Thank you. Could you elaborate on the mental health challenges you mentioned?

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.text: Good question. Research shows that isolation is the biggest concern, particularly for junior staff.

examples.en: Let me turn now to the financial implications.
I'd like to highlight that this represents a 30% increase.
examples.cz: Rád/a bych zdůraznil/a, že to představuje 30% nárůst.

examples.en: If you have any questions, I'd be happy to take them now.
examples.cz: Pokud máte otázky, rád/a je zodpovím.

examples.en: Could you clarify what you mean by 'hybrid'?
examples.cz: Mohl/a byste objasnit, co máte na mysli pod pojmem 'hybridní'?

quiz.sentence: I've ___ my presentation into four sections. (divided)
Let's ___ now to the second point. (move)
To ___ up, the main finding is that prices are rising.
Could you ___ on that last point?

tasks: 1. Prepare a 3-minute presentation on a topic you know well — write the script
2. Write signposting phrases for each stage of your presentation
3. Record yourself presenting for 2 minutes and evaluate: pace, clarity, eye contact
